name: AWS API Gateway
what_it_does: AWS managed API gateway service for creating, deploying, and managing REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs that front Lambda functions, HTTP backends, or AWS services, with usage plans and throttling.
use_cases: Creating serverless API endpoints that invoke Lambda functions from agent workflows
Throttling and rate limiting API access for multi-tenant services
Managing API keys and usage plans for external developer access
WebSocket APIs for real-time agent communication channels
Proxying AWS service calls (DynamoDB, S3, SQS) through a REST API
not_for: Complex API transformations requiring custom plugin ecosystems (use Kong or Apigee)
best_when: You're building serverless applications on AWS and need a fully managed API gateway that integrates natively with Lambda, IAM, and AWS Cognito.
avoid_when: You need multi-cloud portability, complex API transformation policies, or you're not in the AWS ecosystem.

auth.notes: Multiple auth options: IAM (SigV4), Cognito User Pools (JWT), Lambda authorizers (custom), or API keys. JWT authorizers for HTTP APIs. IAM auth is most secure for service-to-service calls.

pricing.free_tier_limits: 1M REST API calls/month free for 12 months; 1M HTTP API calls/month always free
pricing.paid_tiers: REST: $3.50/million calls; HTTP: $1.00/million calls; WebSocket: $1.00/million messages
pricing.notes: HTTP API (v2) is significantly cheaper than REST API (v1) and recommended for most use cases. Data transfer costs apply in addition to API call costs.

performance.rate_limits: Default 10k requests/second, burst 5k; configurable per account

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Management API errors follow AWS standard format (Code, Message). Gateway passthrough errors depend on backend integration. API Gateway-specific errors (throttling, authorizer failures) are documented.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No built-in idempotency for management operations. Deployed APIs are idempotent by resource path — same resource path updates in place. Deployments require explicit deploy action.
known_agent_gotchas: REST API (v1) and HTTP API (v2) have different feature sets — HTTP API is cheaper but lacks usage plans
Changes to API configuration do not take effect until explicitly deployed to a stage
Default account-level throttling limits apply globally — exhausting them affects all APIs
API Gateway has a 29-second integration timeout — long-running Lambda functions will be cut off
VPC integration requires VPC Link setup — adds configuration complexity for private backends
CloudWatch logging for access logs costs extra — monitor log volume in production

security_score_components.security_notes: IAM for management. API Gateway supports IAM auth, Cognito JWT, Lambda authorizers, API keys for API endpoints. WAF integration for request filtering. SOC2, FedRAMP. Resource policies for cross-account access.
